D5 Design L3 Apply Reference

Goethe's Area Balance

a little yellow outweighs a lot of violet — balance complementary colours by area, using Goethe's light-weights

Goethe assigned each hue a "light weight": yellow 9, orange 8, red 6, green 6, blue 4, violet 3. A bright hue carries more visual force, so to balance a pair their areas should be the inverse of their weights — a sliver of yellow against a broad field of violet.

Pick a complementary pair and slide the split. The gold tick marks Goethe's balanced ratio; equal halves (50:50) let the brighter hue shout. Land on the tick and the two feel evenly matched.
